intro: Eugenioides tinctorium, also known as the Yellow Tea Tree or Yellow Eugenia, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Myrtaceae and valued for its medicinal and dye properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eugenioides tinctorium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Eugenioides tinctorium is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are also used in the production of natural dyes and cosmetics.
